# Fish Market / Fishmonger — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-fish-market-fish-market-site
**Vertical slug:** fish-market
**VN-ID:** VN-AGR-MKT-001
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-MKT standalone
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Fish is the most widely consumed animal protein in Nigeria, with per capita consumption estimated at 13kg/year. Nigerian fish markets (Apongbon/Ijora Lagos, Onitsha Fish Market, Badagry, Calabar Seafood Market, Maiduguri) supply fresh, live, smoked, dried stockfish (from Norway), and frozen fish (mackerel/titus, tilapia, catfish, bonga, croaker). The market is dominated by informal traders but formal fish value chain businesses (aquaculture, cold chain, processing) are growing via CBN ABP (Anchor Borrowers Programme). NAFDAC certification is required for processed/packaged fish products. Stockfish (okporoko) trade is culturally significant in Igbo/Delta communities.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Mama Ifeoma, 48, Caterer, Enugu** — Buys 10kg catfish weekly for pepper soup. Needs fresh supply, WhatsApp standing order, delivery to location.
- **Chef Kayode, 35, Hotel Chef, Lagos Island** — Sources 50kg titus weekly. Needs cold chain guarantee, NAFDAC-compliant supplier, bulk invoice.
- **Ngozi Ibe, 29, Supermarket Buyer, Abuja** — Procures frozen fish for shelf. Needs consistent supply, cold storage capacity confirmation, price per carton.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Hero (fresh from water to table), fish types highlight, WhatsApp order CTA, NAFDAC badge
- **Products:** Fresh Fish, Smoked Fish, Dried/Stockfish, Frozen Fish — NGN prices per kg/carton
- **Services:** Bulk supply, delivery, custom smoking/processing
- **About:** Market location, years in trade, NAFDAC registration, associations
- **Contact:** WhatsApp, phone, market stall address, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Fresh, appetising, community-trusted. "Direct from the water to your kitchen" register. Reference iconic markets by name (Apongbon, Onitsha). Primary CTA: "Order on WhatsApp". Secondary CTA: "Get Bulk Price". Avoid clinical/corporate tone — this is a food market voice.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- NAFDAC food safety compliance number
- FMARD fisheries licence
- NIMASA maritime compliance (for artisanal fishing component)
- Cold chain certification (where applicable)
- Market association membership (e.g., Apongbon Fish Dealers Association)
- Customer testimonials (caterers, hotels, supermarkets)
- Payment: bank transfer, POS, cash, mobile money

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Fresh catfish in water tank — live fish market, colourful Lagos waterfront setting
2. Smoked fish (bonga, mackerel) on traditional smoking rack — Delta/Rivers background
3. Market woman displaying dried stockfish — Onitsha-style market stall
4. Cold storage freezer open — frozen titus in cartons, NAFDAC labels visible
5. Chef handling fresh tilapia at hotel kitchen — clean professional setting
6. Delivery of fish in cooler boxes — motorbike delivery, Lagos urban context
7. Fish market at dawn — Apongbon wharf, canoes arriving with catch

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- NAFDAC food safety registration (for smoked/processed/packaged fish)
- FMARD fisheries and aquaculture licence
- NIMASA compliance for fishing vessels
- SON cold chain standards for frozen fish
- NDPR consent in bulk order/delivery forms

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home page and contact page
- [x] All prices in NGN (₦) per kg/carton
- [x] Nigerian markets/cities (Apongbon, Onitsha, Calabar) as location context
- [x] Nigerian names (Mama Ifeoma, Chef Kayode, Ngozi) in testimonials
- [x] NAFDAC trust signal displayed
- [x] Payment: bank transfer, POS, mobile money, cash
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice in order forms
